mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising rate periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor demand and impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off positioning that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.2 percentage points in 1 month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal fails to predict drawdowns in post-2000 data.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in housing starts and retail sales indicate synchronized consumer demand expansion, which lifts forward earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or zero Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window, or equity returns do not significantly outperform when both series rise simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects strong capacity utilization and healthy order backlogs, which translate into higher near-term corporate margins and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns show no statistical difference between rising and falling INDPRO periods, or the signal consistently predicts negative returns during supply booms.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces the Federal Reserve to accelerate policy tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity risk premiums, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral when CPIAUCSL accelerates by the specified threshold, or the signal fails to predict drawdowns in high-inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the historical trend rate confirms expanding aggregate demand, which supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns do not significantly exceed baseline when GDPC1 growth is above trend.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth combined with a stable federal funds rate indicates a soft-landing macroeconomic environment, which optimizes investor risk appetite and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window, or equity returns do not significantly outperform when PAYEMS rises and FEDFUNDS remains flat.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening regime, rising housing starts often precede credit constraints that dampen consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite rising housing starts in a tightening regime, or the signal fails to predict drawdowns when credit spreads widen.
